place: Bellmawr borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Bellmawr became an independent borough on March 23, 1926, after a referendum on April 21 of the same year.
In 2017, Bellmawr joined five other New Jersey towns as one of the first to legalize medical cannabis dispensaries.
The historic Hugg‑Harrison‑Glover House, a patterned‑brick building, stands in Bellmawr’s back‑yard of St. John’s Church as a reminder of the borough’s colonial past.
Bellmawr’s streets have long followed major transportation routes—from flatboats on Little Timber Creek to trains on the Camden County Railroad and today cars between I‑295, I‑76, and Route 42.
